flags.concern: Selection bridge semantics: mirroring local selection with an effect that calls `clearGallerySelection()` on every local-empty transition is incompatible with the current peer-clear contract and will wipe the newly-selected owner. In this repo `selectGalleryItem()` / `selectGalleryItems()` call `peerClearRef.current?.()` before they replace the gallery map, and the existing video-editor peer callback intentionally uses a local-only clear (`clearTimelineOnly`) that does not touch gallery state. If the new timeline/batch bridge clears gallery state when peer-clear empties its local ids, a gallery click or another surface selection will boomerang back and clear the fresh attachments.
flags.evidence: See `src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.tsx:120-178` for the pre-update `peerClearRef.current?.()` calls, and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSelection.ts:159-168` for the existing local-only peer clear registration.

flags.concern: Cross-surface exclusivity: the plan says the generations gallery needs no changes, but gallery multi-select paths do not invoke peer-clear today. `GenerationsPaneGallery` passes `toggle: modifierKeys.isMultiSelectModifier` for Cmd/Ctrl-click, and lasso selection can pass `{ append: true }`; `GallerySelectionContext` only calls `peerClearRef.current?.()` when `toggle`/`append` are false. After wiring timeline/batch selections into the gallery context, additive gallery selection will merge with the other surface instead of clearing it, so the stated mutual-exclusivity behavior is not actually guaranteed.

Batch-selection plumbing: the planned batch bridge reads `selection.selectedIds`, but the mobile batch editor does not use that state. `useSelection()` stores mobile picks in `mobileSelectedIds` whenever `isMobile && generationMode !== 'timeline'`, and `BatchModeContent` always forces `generationMode='batch'` on mobile. Following the plan literally leaves mobile batch selections invisible to `GallerySelectionContext`, so AgentChat attachments still will not appear from that surface on mobile.
